text: Bouaddi is seriously impressive. Not the best quality game. Thought Morocco would go after it a bit more to at least have a two-goal cushion. Scotland were pretty solid but seeing them go down in the box so much was tiring. It seemed like the players didn't believe they were getting a goal from anywhere other than the spot.
label: analysis

text: Two missed penalties against Morocco but somehow the ref caught every flop Morocco made. They're obviously more skilled but somehow feel they need to resort to dirty plays and flops.

text: Morocco should be playing Bouaddi more in center than DM. He can control the game better from there. Feel like he is wasted deep and Morocco are unnecessarily playing defensive when not needed.
label: analysis

text: USA's midfield is just running circles around them right now. Australia's got decent shape at the back but they're getting picked apart in transition. Seen this pattern before where a team sits deep early and gets caught out because they can't win the ball back quickly enough. If the Aussies want to stay in this they need someone pressing higher.
label: analysis
Notes: Detailed tactical analysis involving midfield control, transitions, pressing, and defensive shape.

text: USA dropping $6 million and making Pochettino the third highest-paid national team coach in the world is looking like a shrewd investment.

text: These Aussies are not the same ones we played against bruv. Wtf is this performance.

text: Australia playing badly, especially their defenders. Their passes are constantly being intercepted by the USA midfield. America is playing a dominant game.

text: I genuinely feel right now Colombia could be the second-best team in South America. Based on what I saw in Copa America 2024, they have the personnel to make a good run. text: Brazil was so disappointing to watch. The attack was good in the first half but in the second half they focused on defending and missed good chances. Their midfield and defense are very weak. If they want to win the World Cup, they need Neymar and need to improve midfield and defense.

text: They were getting killed on the counter by Morocco. Raphinha needs to step it up. Vini had a good first match to build from. I loved seeing him track back and his goal too.

text: Paqueta was a real weak link, in the first half it looked like he was playing with Morocco against Brazil. Even when he received the ball alone he lost it with bad passes or poor control. Also Carlo played the first half without anyone operating on the right side, which was shocking. Endrick should have started on the right. Hopefully Carlo learns from that and improves the team during the World Cup.
label: analysis
Notes: Discusses player performance, tactical setup, positional issues, and lineup decisions with supporting observations.

text: Vini was pants outside of the wonder goal he scored. He miscontrolled 4-5 passes, Hakimi had him all game. Brazil looked better with Fabinho in midfield and Cunha in attack. Morocco looked balanced and organized but lacked a killer instinct.

text: I thought France was going to be Spain from yesterday with the way they couldn't convert the open chances, but luckily they got their guard up. Senegal should have made the substitutions better, Mbaye should have been brought on much earlier. France's passing was quite horrible in the first half, but the team stepped up in the second. Senegal could have put a lot of pressure on France and should have taken advantage of the opportunity.
label: analysis
Notes: Discusses chance conversion, substitutions, passing quality, game-state changes, and tactical opportunities. Strong football reasoning throughout.
